title: Meghan O'Sullivan on Office Hours
summary: Meghan O’Sullivan, Jeane Kirkpatrick Professor of the Practice of International Affairs at the Harvard Kennedy School and author of 'Windfall: How the New Energy Abundance Upends Global Politics and Strengthens America’s Power,' talks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about fracking, America’s strategic petroleum reserve, energy abundance, and her early start in geopolitics.
body: Gallery 1 of 4 Meghan O'Sullivan: Defining Tight Oil 2 of 4 Meghan O’Sullivan: My Early Fascination with Landlocked Countries 3 of 4 Meghan O'Sullivan: Fracking, Solar, Wind, and Natural Gas 4 of 4 Meghan O'Sullivan: Trump’s Risky Business on the Strategic Petroleum Reserve Meghan L. O’Sullivan is the Jeane Kirkpatrick Professor of the Practice of International Affairs and Director of the Geopolitics of Energy Project at Harvard University’s Kennedy School. Her expertise includes the geopolitics of energy, decision making in foreign policy, nation-building, counterinsurgency, and the Middle East. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article features Meghan L. O'Sullivan, the Jeane Kirkpatrick Professor at Harvard Kennedy School and Director of the Geopolitics of Energy Project. It highlights her expertise in the geopolitics of energy, foreign policy decision-making, nation-building, counterinsurgency, and the Middle East. The content references specific topics she has addressed, including defining tight oil, her interest in landlocked countries, the roles of fracking, solar, wind, and natural gas, and critiques of Trump's policies regarding the Strategic Petroleum Reserve. The piece situates her work within broader discussions on energy security, market dynamics, and the intersection of energy policy with international relations and geopolitical strategy.
result.main_content_zh: 本文重点介绍了哈佛大学肯尼迪学院珍妮·柯克帕特里克实践教授兼能源地缘政治项目主任梅根·L·奥沙利文。文章强调了她在能源地缘政治、外交政策决策、国家建设、反叛乱和中东事务方面的专业知识。内容提及了她探讨的具体议题，包括致密油的定义、对内陆国家的早期兴趣、水力压裂、太阳能、风能和天然气的作用，以及对特朗普政府战略石油储备政策的批评。文章将她的工作置于更广泛的讨论中，涉及能源安全、市场动态以及能源政策与国际关系、地缘政治战略的交集。
